title: Case Converter
shortDescription: Convert text into uppercase, lowercase, title case, sentence case, and more with clear results for pasted text or typed input.
metaDescription: Change text to uppercase, lowercase, title case, or sentence case with a clean browser-based converter.
intro: Paste or type text, then switch it to uppercase, lowercase, title case, or sentence case in one step.
overview:
This tool rewrites your text into the case style you choose. It is useful when a headline needs standard capitalization, a draft needs consistent formatting, or copied text needs to match a house style.

You can test short phrases, full sentences, headings, and longer passages. The output updates from the exact characters you enter, so punctuation, spacing, and line breaks stay part of the result unless the selected case rule changes them.

Use it when you need a fast formatting pass before publishing, pasting into a document, or sharing text with a cleaner presentation.
howItWorks: Enter or paste the text you want to change.
Choose the target case style from the available options.
Copy the converted result and use it wherever you need.

faq.question: Does this tool change only letters, or does it also touch numbers and symbols?
faq.answer: Numbers and symbols are kept as they are. Only the letter casing changes according to the option you choose.

faq.question: Will title case capitalize every word the same way?
faq.answer: Title case follows the tool's casing rules for words in the text. If you need a strict publication style, review names, abbreviations, and small words after converting.

faq.question: What happens to line breaks and spacing?
faq.answer: The text structure is preserved as entered unless the selected case mode requires a different treatment. Extra spaces already in the input may still affect the result.

faq.question: Why did my acronym or brand name change?
faq.answer: Case conversion applies the chosen rule to the text, so all-caps brands, acronyms, or special styling may be altered. Edit those terms manually if they must stay exact.
